title: Ernek Marskan
content:
{{Top|ga|leg}} {{Character |type=Rebel |image= |name=Ernek Marskan |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species= |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]]''[[X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide]]'' |pronouns=He/him |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings=[[Casal Marskan]] |children= |affiliation=[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|You killed my brother!|Ernek Marskan, to Keyan Farlander|}} '''Ernek Marskan''' was a [[Captain/Legends|captain]] in the [[Alliance Starfighter Corps/Legends|Alliance Starfighter Corps]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] by [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]]. He served as a [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] with [[Blue Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Blue Squadron]] on the [[Mon Calamari Star Cruiser/Legends|Mon Calamari Star Cruiser]] [[Independence/Legends|''Independence'']], participating in many missions by that [[Standard year/Legends|year]]. When his younger brother, [[Casal Marskan|Casal]], was killed during a [[Skirmish in the Horuz system|mission]] with fellow pilot [[Keyan Farlander/Legends|Keyan Farlander]], Marskan blamed Farlander for the [[Death/Legends|death]]. However, when Marskan confronted the other pilot, Farlander used [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to [[Telekinesis/Legends|choke]] him. The incident led to animosity toward Farlander from the other pilots. Marskan was later one of several pilots transferred to [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] for the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|battle]] against the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] and among the few pilots to survive. ==Biography== ===Alliance service=== Ernek Marskan was the elder brother of [[Rebel pilot/Legends|Rebel pilot]] [[Casal Marskan]]. He joined the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] early in the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], preceding his brother into the [[Alliance Starfighter Corps/Legends|Alliance Starfighter Corps]]. By [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]],''[[X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide]]'' begins after the [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing starfighter]] entered service with the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], which ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' places in [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]]. ''X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide'' establishes that the [[Battle of Tatooine (Galactic Civil War)|capture of Leia Organa]] occurs at the end of [[Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)|''Star Wars: X-Wing'']]{{'s}} second tour of duty. Organa's capture takes place on 35:3:3 under the [[Great ReSynchronization]] dating system, placing it at the beginning of [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]] according to [[Template:YearMathGRS|the following explanation]]. Events in ''X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide'' before the end of the second tour of duty therefore take place in 1 BBY. Marskan had achieved the rank of [[Captain/Legends|captain]] and was a senior [[Flight leader/Legends|flight leader]] in [[Blue Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Blue Squadron]] aboard the [[Mon Calamari Star Cruiser/Legends|Mon Calamari Star Cruiser]] [[Independence/Legends|''Independence'']], though he flew with other [[Squadron/Legends|squadrons]] when needed. After the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] launched [[Operation Strike Fear]] in response to the Rebel victory at the [[Battle of Turkana/Legends|Battle of Turkana]], Marskan was one of several officers involved in the early planning for a Rebel operation to destroy the [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Invincible (Strike Fear)|''Invincible'']]. The Alliance had learned of a powerful Imperial explosive device being transported aboard the [[BFF-1 bulk freighter/Legends|BFF-1 bulk freighter]] ''[[Gafra]]'' to the [[Celanon/Legends|Celanon]] [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]] and was planning a raid to capture it for use against the ''Invincible''. Marskan was concerned that Celanon was too well defended for a raid to succeed. His opinion was shared by the ''Independence''{{'s}} mission planner, [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[H. S'man|S'man]], who decided to launch the raid at a [[Realspace/Legends|deep-space]] transfer zone along the ''Gafra''{{'s}} course to Celanon. The mission was a success, and the Rebels were later able to smuggle the explosive aboard the [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] and detonate it, [[Destruction of the Invincible|destroying]] the vessel.[[Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)|''Star Wars: X-Wing'']] [[File:MarskanFarlanderGunboat.png|thumb|left|270px|Marskan and Farlander pursue an [[Alpha-class Xg-1 Star Wing/Legends|Assault Gunboat]].]] Following the defeat of Operation Strike Fear, as the Rebels searched for more information about the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]], the ''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer [[Intrepid (Imperial I-class)|''Intrepid'']]{{'s}} [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] was damaged in an accidental collision with an [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate]], leaving it stranded in the [[Circarpous system/Legends|Circarpous system]]. A Rebel team of [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|BTL Y-wing starfighters]], led by [[Keyan Farlander/Legends|Keyan Farlander]] and escorted by Marskan in a [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighter]], were sent to [[Battle of the Circarpous system (Galactic Civil War)|destroy]] a group of Imperial reinforcements before they could reach the Star Destroyer. Marskan arrived late and his performance was reckless, frequently targeting the same [[Starship/Legends|ships]] as the Y-wings, putting his allies in danger. The mission was a success, nevertheless, allowing the Rebels to mount a subsequent attack that destroyed the Star Destroyer. However, following Marskan's poor performance, the ''Independence''{{'s}} briefing officer, Commander [[Lagrane]], promised Farlander that Marskan would receive an official reprimand, but Farlander asked him to go easy on the [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] as everybody makes mistakes. Shortly before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]],''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' Marskan was one of several pilots in the ready room aboard the ''Independence'' when Farlander entered, having lost his [[Wingmate/Legends|wingmate]], [[Breth Gart]], in a previous [[Battle of Kalla VII|mission]]. Captain [[Samuel Raider]] went to console Farlander and was clearly surprised when the other replied that Gart simply hadn't been strong enough to survive. The incident was one of the first signs of a growing change in Farlander. The pilot quickly became increasingly militant, taking a group of rookie pilots under his wing and encouraging them to act aggressively in combat. Among his followers was Marskan's younger brother, Casal, having recently followed Marskan into the Alliance. ===Casal's death and Battle of Yavin=== [[File:FarlanderYavin-ANH.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Farlander's Y-wing in the Battle of Yavin, where Ernek Marskan also participated and both pilots survived]] Following Cassal's [[Death/Legends|death]] flying a [[Skirmish in the Horuz system|mission]] with Farlander a few days later, Marskan blamed the other for his brother's loss. He confronted Farlander as he emerged from a debriefing, shoving him against a wall, gripping his throat, and moving to strike him. Farlander used [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to make Marskan release him, before closing his windpipe and [[Telekinesis/Legends|choking]] him. Marskan clutched uselessly at his throat before Farlander, shocked by his own actions, finally released him. Scared and angry, Marskan fled to the ready room, where he told several other pilots, including Raider and [[Speedy]], what had happened, creating hostility toward Farlander from the others. Shortly after Marskan's confrontation with Farlander, the ''Independence'' traveled to the Alliance's [[Great Temple/Legends|base]] on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] to assist in the preparations for an expected attack from the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]]. Marskan, along with several other pilots including Raider and Farlander, was transferred to Yavin Base upon arrival. The Death Star arrived in the [[Yavin system/Legends|Yavin system]] soon after the Rebels, but the Alliance pilots succeeded in defeating the Imperial [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]] due to the efforts of the rookie pilot [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]. Marskan and Farlander were among the battle's survivors. However, despite Farlander apologizing for his actions, Marskan continued to hate him for his part in Casal's death. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|Listen, commander…Marskan's a good pilot…I just hope he's OK.''"
"''I would think you'd be pretty angry with him.''"
"''I am. But I also know that we all make mistakes.|Farlander and Lagrane discuss Marskan|}} While friendly with the other pilots aboard the ''Independence'', Ernek Marskan's relationship with Farlander was strained by the other pilot's role in his brother's death, which left Marskan angry to the point where he attacked Farlander, though Farlander's use of the Force easily defeated him. ==Skills and abilities== Marskan was a competent pilot who, despite at least one reckless performance, was generally considered reliable in combat. ==Behind the scenes== {{Quote|''[…]'' Red 12 finally gets a name, chosen after sorting through a number of candidates. They were Naeco (original ''X-wing'' game), Captain Ernek Marskan (same), Fin Danglot (''Galaxy Guide 1''), Travis (a blonde woman from Marvel's retelling of ''A New Hope'' back in the ''Droids'' kid's comic – how's ''that'' for obscure?), and Talos Merkin (''Captive to Evil'').|Jason Fry, ''The Essential Guide to Warfare'' endnotes|{{Tumblr|subdomain=jasonfry|url=post/24614452839/eg-to-warfare-endnotes-pt-10|name=Jason Fry's Dorkery|text=EG to Warfare: Endnotes Pt. 10|quote=[…] Red 12 finally gets a name, chosen after sorting through a number of candidates. They were Naeco (original ''X-wing'' game), Captain Ernek Marskan (same), Fin Danglot (''Galaxy Guide 1''), Travis (a blonde woman from Marvel's retelling of ''A New Hope'' back in the ''Droids'' kid's comic – how's ''that'' for obscure?), and Talos Merkin (''Captive to Evil'').}}}} Ernek Marskan was created by [[Rusel DeMaria]] for the [[1993]] book ''[[X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide]]'', which presented the story of Keyan Farlander, the player character in the game [[Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)|''Star Wars: X-Wing'']]. In the book, Marskan is established as one of the previously unidentified pilots aboard the ''Independence''. While writing the [[2012]] reference book, ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'', author [[Jason Fry]] considered Marskan as one of several candidates for the role of [[Red Twelve/Legends|Red Twelve]], a previously unidentified Rebel pilot whose death at the Battle of Yavin is depicted in [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']]. Fry and [[Leland Chee]], keeper of the [[Holocron continuity database]] ultimately rejected Marskan in favor of another character from ''X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide'', [[Puck Naeco/Legends|Puck Naeco]].{{Tumblr|subdomain=jasonfry|url=post/24614452839/eg-to-warfare-endnotes-pt-10|name=Jason Fry's Dorkery|text=EG to Warfare: Endnotes Pt. 10|quote=[…] Red 12 finally gets a name, chosen after sorting through a number of candidates. They were Naeco (original ''X-wing'' game), Captain Ernek Marskan (same), Fin Danglot (''Galaxy Guide 1''), Travis (a blonde woman from Marvel's retelling of ''A New Hope'' back in the ''Droids'' kid's comic – how's ''that'' for obscure?), and Talos Merkin (''Captive to Evil'').}} ''X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide'' had previously established that Marskan survived the Battle of Yavin. ==Appearances== *[[Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)|''Star Wars: X-Wing'']] {{1st}} {{C|Fighter only}} ==Sources== *''[[X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide]]'' {{1stID|Ernek Marskan}} *''[[X-Wing Collector's CD-ROM: The Official Strategy Guide]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Marskan, Ernek}} [[Category:Blue Squadron personnel]] [[Category:Individuals of unspecified species]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Males]]
